newsSince: No new company-specific catalyst since June 9. The standing flow remains the CES 2026 NVIDIA-powered AI radar platform, a Hirain LRR610 Level-4 design win in China (SOP Dec 2026), and a Q1 2026 revenue jump off a tiny base. The -18% move since June 9 is micro-cap noise with no negative news identified.

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid-stack lens: ARBE is radar-perception optionality, not a humanoid supplier. NVIDIA platform + China L4 design win keep the redundant-sensing thesis alive, but no fresh catalyst since June 9 and ~$90M cap on tiny revenue. Hold; slight trim for unchanged-yet-rich speculative beta.
verdicts.bull: Radar redundancy becomes essential as robots/AVs leave controlled demos; NVIDIA platform + China L4 win seed the chipset funnel.
verdicts.neutral: Real design wins but revenue still negligible; story-stock optionality at a sub-$100M cap.
verdicts.bear: No humanoid traction yet, cash burn, dilution risk; -42% YTD signals fading patience.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Conversion of NVIDIA/Hirain design wins into chipset SOP volume (Dec 2026).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cash burn and dilution at a micro-cap with negligible revenue.
